title: Linear interpolation preserves constant data

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 8 (Polynomial Interpolation).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: On a nondegenerate interval, the two-point linear interpolant of constant endpoint data is constant at every evaluation point.
informal.expected_conclusion: The linear interpolant of constant data is constant.

declarations.name: linearInterp
declarations.statement: def linearInterp (x0 x1 y0 y1 x : Real) : Real := ((x1 - x) / (x1 - x0)) * y0 + ((x - x0) / (x1 - x0)) * y1

declarations.name: linearInterp_const
declarations.statement: theorem linearInterp_const (x0 x1 c x : Real) (h : x1 - x0 ≠ 0) : linearInterp x0 x1 c c x = c

tasks.input: Translate the interpolation theorem `linearInterp_const` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem linearInterp_const (x0 x1 c x : Real) (h : x1 - x0 ≠ 0) : linearInterp x0 x1 c c x = c

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the interpolation proof.
